userMessage: add a few more relvent measuremnts

response.text: Done — The scope is showing an eye-diagram analysis of Ref1 for a high-speed NRZ serial signal at about 2.5008 Gb/s. The main eye plot is fairly open but visibly narrowed by deterministic jitter, with eye width about 345.9 ps and midlevel eye height about 80.36 mV. The jitter summary reports TIE(σ) about 8.68 ps, total jitter about 54.07 ps, random jitter about 1.68 ps, and deterministic jitter about 33.75 ps, so deterministic effects dominate. The TIE histogram is asymmetric/multi-lobed, the TIE spectrum shows strong discrete components, and the bathtub curve is displayed for BER margin. The lower waveform view shows the raw signal centered near 0 V with roughly ±550 mV swing. The added measurements on the right read Period about 1.066 ns, Frequency about 1.106 GHz, Amplitude about 744.8 mV, Peak-to-Peak about 1.104 V, Rise Time about 145.4 ps, and Fall Time about 220.4 ps. Overall, it looks like a working multi-level-appearing overlaid serial eye with modest vertical margin and jitter dominated by data-dependent/channel-related effects.
